steps: An exponential growth function has the form A(t) = A_0 \cdot r^t where A_0 is the initial value. The account opens with $35,600.00, so A_0 = 35,600.00. This eliminates choices C and D.
The balance grows from $35,600 to $129,672.38 over 15 years, so r > 1 (growth). A rate of 0.09 < 1 would cause decay, not growth. This eliminates choice B.
Verify choice A: A(15) = 35,600(1.09)^{15}. Since (1.09)^{15} \approx 3.6425, we get 35,600 \times 3.6425 \approx 129,672, matching the given value.
explanation:
• Choice A — '$A(t) = 35,600.00(1.09)^t$': Correct initial value $35,600 and growth factor 1.09 (9% annual growth). Verified: 35,600 × (1.09)^15 ≈ $129,672.38. ✓
  • Choice B — '$A(t) = 35,600.00(0.09)^t$': Correct initial value but r = 0.09 < 1 causes exponential decay. After 15 years the balance would be essentially $0, not $129,672. ✗
  • Choice C — '$A(t) = 94,072.38(1.09)^t$': The initial value $94,072.38 is the increase ($129,672.38 - $35,600), not the starting balance. At t = 0 the account balance should be $35,600. ✗
  • Choice D — '$A(t) = 94,072.38(0.09)^t$': Wrong initial value and decay factor. Both errors disqualify this choice. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
